name: Formatting Brain
purpose: Control page flow, spacing, typography, title pages, and printable assessment layout without changing mathematical content.
rules: Treat space blocks as intentional working area, not generic page filler.
Do not add a trailing question-level space block immediately before a manual page break; leftover page area already provides working room.
Keep space blocks inside parts or subparts when they are intended answer space for that specific part.
Be generous with student answer space when authoring assessments. The student copy should not feel cramped, and extra student space is acceptable when it helps the matching solution slot stay stable.

As a sizing heuristic, give short one-mark free-response items at least a small answer space, routine two- or three-mark items enough room for several working lines, and multi-step items enough room for method plus final answer. Prefer a little too much student space over a cramped response area.
Do not remove or shrink student answer space simply because the solution is short. The student copy is allowed to have more room than the solution uses.
For shared student/solution documents, newly created response Space blocks should be explicit student answer/work spaces and immediately adjacent to the worked-solution module that replaces them.

New paired answer/solution slots should default to blank response space with showLines false. Only turn on ruled lines when the teacher requests lines or an existing source/layout deliberately uses them.

If the solution copy still shows a student answer-space block, treat it as an unpaired missing-solution signal. Add or repair the adjacent solution module rather than deleting the answer space or calling the layout done.
Existing or imported plain Space blocks with no visibility field are shared ordinary blocks until deliberately converted; do not infer student-only visibility from kind: "space" alone.
Ordinary question modules should remain shared between the student copy and solution copy; only student answer spaces and worked-solution modules should change across the solution toggle.

A blank table used for student completion is an answer surface and may be student-only in a matched solution slot. A table containing given question data is ordinary shared content and should stay visible in both copies.
In completed solution tables, preserve normal table grid, row/column labels, and given-value styling. Only cells corresponding to blank student-entry cells should use the solution colour.
Table cell content should be vertically centred by default. Use table alignment controls for horizontal placement rather than spacing text manually inside cells.
Use tables as plain grids for now: the first row is ordinary content, not a special header row.

If a worked solution needs more reserved room than the current answer space provides, increase the relevant answer-space block lines rather than adding unrelated blank space or changing page flow in only one copy.

When a solution slot needs more room, first use compact solution formatting and side-by-side diagram placement where appropriate; if it still does not fit, increase the matching answer-space block rather than making the solution copy taller on its own.

A student answer-space block immediately after a left- or right-aligned diagram is rendered as one diagram response slot: the student copy shows an L-shaped side-and-under working area, and the solution copy fills the same slot with solution content that wraps beside the diagram and continues underneath. Use one space block after the diagram; do not fake the L-shape with multiple unrelated spaces.

After mauth.author.adjustResponseSpaces, post-edit inspection may return rendered-response-space-outline-missing, rendered-solution-space-overflow, or rendered-page-overflow before success is reported. Repair by adjusting the same paired answer-space block or nearby page flow; preserve existing wording, diagrams, solution modules, ids, and ordering.

Treat rendered-page-overflow as browser-measured evidence for the named Student or Solutions preview. After changing the document, wait for that copy to render and read a fresh snapshot before claiming the warning is resolved; do not reuse the prior document's warning or treat it as a solution-completeness failure.

For focused formatting requests in the external agent, use mauth.format.apply rather than replacing the whole question or hand-building low-level JSON. Use setPageBreakBefore/removePageBreakBefore for page breaks, setDiagramAlignment for left/center/right diagram placement, adjustAnswerSpace for line counts, moveModule for known module moves, fitSolutionToSpace for solution-fit repairs, and tidyQuestionSpacing for conservative spacing cleanup.

For selected/current module settings requests, use mauth.settings.apply instead of replacing a question or asking the user for raw ids. It can use the active selected module or selected geometry2d primitive and maps to module.settings.update and diagram.settings.update internally.

For focused low-level module setting edits in a broader action batch, use module.settings.update instead of raw module.update patches when changing space line counts, ruled-line visibility, table rows/columns/alignment, column counts, choices layout, or diagram module alignment.

After mauth.format.apply, post-edit inspection may return formatting-layout warnings before success is reported. Repair once with the same high-level formatting tool or the matching response-space/solution tool; preserve existing question wording, diagrams, solution modules, ids, and ordering.

When the external agent is asked to check the whole document layout, print readiness, blank pages, oversized diagrams, missing answer spaces, solution-space fit, or whether the document is ready to print, use mauth.layout.check with autoRepair: true. Use mode "student" for student-copy checks, "solutions" for solution-copy checks, and "both" when the teacher asks for the whole document or print readiness.

mauth.layout.check is a document-wide inspection tool that may repair safe mechanical layout issues once when autoRepair is true: missing student answer spaces, oversized editable diagrams, final blank-page breaks, rendered page overflow from one clearly identified student space block, and one rendered clipped/oversized diagram only when fresh preview metrics identify exactly one editable diagram target. Treat remaining issues as repair instructions: use mauth.format.apply for page-break, spacing, alignment, and move repairs; mauth.author.adjustResponseSpaces for answer-space sizing; mauth.author.ensureSolutions or mauth.solutions.writeAll for solution coverage/tick repairs; and diagram tools for multiple, ambiguous, oversized, clipped, or failed diagrams.
Respect manual author overrides for answer-space line counts, ruled-line visibility, diagram alignment, and diagram side settings unless they produce an obvious layout error.

For school exam booklets, use one question per printed page as the default loose layout rule. Add a question-boundary page break after each question except the final question unless the question naturally needs to continue onto a second page.

For school exam booklets, size each question's own part/subpart answer-space blocks from the mark value and task type so the whole question usually fits on one printed page. After the question content fits, distribute most remaining page space across the existing part/subpart answer spaces, weighted toward parts worth more marks, so the page feels intentionally used without adding unrelated trailing filler.

When formatting a test or exam, inspect spare working capacity page by page and explicitly adjust the existing answer-space line counts on that page. Share usable whole-line additions in proportion to the marks represented by those spaces, use equal judgement when marks are unavailable, conceptually floor proportional shares so the edits cannot exceed observed capacity, and leave uncertain remainder at the page bottom. Preserve teacher overrides and verify Student and Solutions after the edits. This is an agent authoring philosophy, not an automatic renderer feature.
Questions may span pages, but avoid splitting a part or subpart when there is a reasonable alternative.
Use manual page breaks for deliberate section/page starts, not for small cosmetic gaps.

Use mini-TOC question page breaks for boundaries between questions. When a long question must split at a deliberate point, use an explicit editor Page break divider row before the relevant part or subpart; in the stored data this is pageBreakBefore on the following part/subpart. Do not add standalone page-break content blocks inside a question.

Use $...$ for inline math and $$...$$ for display math. This applies to question text, tables, labels, and title-page/front-matter text fields. Do not emit \(...\) or \[...\] delimiters because the Mauth text renderer expects dollar-delimited MathJax.

Write simple prose values as normal text rather than LaTeX: use 7%, 15, 18 months, 5.7% p.a., and 2024 to 2025 unless the value is part of a real formula, equation, coordinate, variable statement, symbolic table heading, or mathematical answer option in a choices block.

When converting source exams, preserve the source math flow: if a tall vector, matrix, fraction, or equation appears inline in a sentence, keep it inline with $...$ rather than changing it to $$...$$. Use display math only for equations that are standalone blocks in the original or need a deliberate displayed layout.

Preserve meaningful source line breaks and paragraph breaks in question text. If the original puts a second sentence or instruction on the next line, keep that as a newline inside the same text block unless it should be a separate structural module.

When converting attached screenshots, PDFs, Word documents, or text-like source files, treat the source layout as the formatting source of truth. Preserve deliberate line breaks, table/diagram alignment, marks placement, and pagination when the teacher asks for fidelity, but still use structured Mauth modules rather than screenshot-like text spacing.

When the teacher asks for a screenshot/source question with the diagram underneath and the parts under the diagram, structure it as stem text, then a question-level diagram block, then structured parts. Do not create a prose diagram paragraph in place of the diagram, and do not leave blank part rows.
Math is rendered through MathJax SVG. The renderer applies \displaystyle to inline math by default, so inline formulas stay in the sentence while fractions and operators use display-style sizing.
The inline \displaystyle behaviour is a local wrapper rather than MathJax display mode; MathJax display mode creates block layout and should not be used for inline text.

For vertical systems or grouped equations inside display math, use supported MathJax environments such as \begin{aligned}...\end{aligned}; do not emit \begin{aligned*}. If rows look cramped, add a small optional row gap such as \\[2pt] or \\[3pt] between lines.

TeX still uses smaller math styles inside fraction numerators and denominators. For printable assessment content, write nested large expressions explicitly, for example \frac{\displaystyle\binom{n}{r}p^r(1-p)^{n-r}}{\displaystyle\sum_{x=0}^{n} ...}, so binomial coefficients, sums, products, and similar notation do not shrink inside fractions.
When editing title-page assessment titles, preserve LaTeX commands and delimiters exactly; only normal prose outside math should be uppercased by the title-page style.

Use frontMatter.titlePageTemplate to choose the title model. Use "standard" for the normal school-test title page, "exam" for the single supported school exam booklet style, "worksheet" for a compact first-page heading with questions starting immediately underneath, "notes" for printable mathematics notes, and "investigation" for a non-question investigation brief with a linked teacher rubric.

For a standard test with multiple named sections, insert a top-level section marker before every section, including the first. The leading marker supplies the section title and section-only mark total on the opening test title page; each later marker generates another full test title page. Section markers are structural and are not repeated as inline headings above the questions. Each title page shows only the marks from its following questions up to the next marker or the end of the document.

For standard-test section title pages, keep logo, school name, subject title, main assessment title, and starting question number shared in frontMatter. Use the section heading title as that page's subtitle and its titlePage settings only for page-specific name or mark labels, declaration, and instructions. Never store a manual section mark total; derive it from the following questions.

For investigations, use frontMatter.titlePageTemplate = "investigation" and formattingConfig.id = "investigation". Keep questions, parts, solution slots, and section markers empty. Store the administration fields, task, shared criterion headings and guidance, and detailed teacher allocation rows under frontMatter.investigation.

Render the investigation Student copy as one shared standard-test title page ordered as title identity, Name/Result row, then task and general marking guidance. Do not place task content between the title and student row or add a repeated brief header, logo, or administration grid. Teacher mode retains that student page and adds as many compact rubric pages as the detailed criteria need. Repeat the rubric context, title, and column headings on each rubric page, show instructions on the first, and show the final total only on the last. Use additive criteria for independent mark allocations and holistic criteria for alternative performance levels; a holistic 4/3/2/1 scale contributes 4 marks, not 10. Derive the result total from the structured criteria and do not duplicate the rubric as question content or ordinary prose.
For worksheets, use frontMatter.titlePageTemplate = "worksheet" and formattingConfig.id = "worksheet". The worksheet title is not a separate page.

Worksheet headings use the selected mini logo, school name, assessment title, subject title, the bottom heading rule as the name area, and a mark field only when marks exist. Do not use frontMatter.assessmentSubtitle for worksheets; put extra worksheet context in the title or normal question/text content.
For worksheet column layouts, use normal :::columns blocks inside the relevant question, part, or subpart. Do not use document-level worksheet column settings.

For worksheet source sections such as Multiple choice and Short answer, use top-level section headings in sectionHeadings/documentFlow via sectionHeading actions. Do not fake section titles as zero-mark questions or as the first text module inside a question.

For worksheet multiple-choice questions, prefer choices layout "inline" when short options can fit on one row; layout "two-column" also renders as a compact wrapped row in worksheet preview when it fits. If a choices block mixes algebraic options with simple numeric options, keep the numeric options in inline math too, such as $18$, $-3$, and $24$, so option typography stays consistent.

For exam-style papers, use frontMatter.exam fields for the school-logo cover, exam heading, booklet title, student name line, running course/section headers, time allowed, materials, important note, structure table, instructions, question-page footers, and supplementary pages. Do not fake these with ordinary question text modules.

For exam-style papers, newly authored free-response questions should normally have a question page break after them and generous answer-space line counts so students have room to work. Do not compress multiple questions onto one page simply because the question text is short.

For school exam booklets, set frontMatter.exam.sectionPreset to "section-one-calculator-free" or "section-two-calculator-assumed". Section One uses CALCULATOR-FREE, five minutes reading time, fifty minutes working time, and nil special items. Section Two uses CALCULATOR-ASSUMED, ten minutes reading time, one hundred minutes working time, formula sheet retained from Section One, and calculator/CAS special items.

For exam structure tables, set useCurrentDocument: true on the row representing the current Mauth document. That row automatically uses the current question count and total marks in preview/print output. Leave companion sections, such as a second calculator-assumed paper, as manual rows unless the user explicitly supplies or selects the companion section file.
Do not silently infer a sister exam file from a filename when filling structure-table rows. Future linked-companion support should be explicit, visible, and manually overrideable.
The exam structure percentage column is deliberate metadata and may be typed manually even when marks and question counts are automated.
Exam booklet page counts are padded automatically to a multiple of four at render/print time. Do not add fake blank question pages, page-break modules, or text modules to force booklet pagination.

Renderer-created exam booklet supplementary pages are not editable content. They use the school-exam supplementary page layout with a running header, Supplementary page title, and Question number line.

When creating a new document, choose the document template first: school tests use titlePageTemplate "standard" with format preset "high-school-mathematics-test"; worksheets use titlePageTemplate "worksheet" with format preset "worksheet"; school exam booklets use titlePageTemplate "exam" with format preset "exam-booklet"; mathematics notes use titlePageTemplate "notes" with format preset "math-notes"; investigations use titlePageTemplate "investigation" with format preset "investigation".
If a specific inline formula needs compact MathJax sizing, start it with \textstyle.
Keep marks displayed in parentheses, for example (1 mark) and (3 marks).
Keep the main question text at the standard test font size unless the title page or a diagram/chart style explicitly requires otherwise.
Use the standard test paragraph line height with modest breathing room; do not compact ordinary question text so tightly that adjacent lines or inline maths feel cramped.
Prefer centered tables and diagrams unless the question context benefits from left or right alignment.

For diagrams aligned left or right, the renderer automatically places the next text block, answer-space block, or student/solution replacement slot on the opposite side when adjacent content follows. Do not ask the user to set a separate text-side control for ordinary assessment layouts.

Keep side-by-side diagram/text pairs compact; preserve the diagram's original left or right alignment and do not let a flexible text column push the diagram to the far edge and create a large empty gutter.

When solution working should sit beside a given diagram or solution-only diagram, rely on the automatic diagram response slot instead of faking wrap with blank lines, manual spacing, or duplicated content. The answer/solution content should use the side space first and then continue underneath the diagram at full width if needed.
In solution copies, solution text blocks should read as answer-key content: compact spacing, clear alignment with the relevant question/part/subpart content, and no decorative framing.
When a solution follows a labelled part or subpart, align the word Solution with the start of that part's mathematical content rather than with the part label column.
Give a small amount of vertical breathing room before a solution block after a tall display equation, integral, table, chart, or diagram.
In solution copies, render [[marks:n]] annotations as n large red check marks at the right edge of the annotated line; do not show the raw annotation text.
PDF output should use the browser print path from the same generated A4 preview pages as the visible test preview.
For print CSS, let @page own the physical A4 sheet size and margins. Generated app pages should render as printable content areas, not full physical-height paper boxes in the DOM.
Do not reintroduce print-time render scaling, full-page DOM clipping, or browser-specific print branches unless a narrow compatibility issue is proven and documented.

Preview zoom should use the normal A4 page layout scale directly. During wheel/pinch gestures, update the A4 preview CSS scale variables imperatively, keep the document point under the cursor anchored in both axes, coalesce scroll corrections into animation frames, and sync React state after the gesture settles; do not rerender the full paginated document on every input tick. Do not add temporary high-resolution, cached, or transform-only zoom wrappers around the preview; display-only maximum zoom should land at native A4 pixel scale rather than magnifying the source render.
Screen preview page-frame performance hints such as content-visibility must stay screen-only; never let them affect the print-preview stage or generated PDF output.
Whole-document structure settings live in formattingConfig and are edited in the document format panel under T. Normal UI can edit mark-label visibility.

Do not expose a normal document-style dropdown that changes formattingConfig.id independently of frontMatter.titlePageTemplate. The format preset should stay tied to the chosen document template; use formatting.update for preset repair only when the stored template and format are already inconsistent.
Use pageFormat.update only for explicit advanced page repairs or imported-file compatibility. Do not expose page width, height, margins, or page-break-label controls as normal worksheet/test UI.

For exam booklets, use frontMatter.titlePageTemplate = "exam" and formattingConfig.id = "exam-booklet". Store all cover-page, running-header, footer, instruction, structure-table, and supplementary-page data in frontMatter.exam rather than ordinary question text.

When converting a past exam section, choose the matching exam section preset, set the structure table row for the open section to useCurrentDocument: true so question count and marks come from the current Mauth document, and enter other source-paper sections manually in frontMatter.exam.structureRows unless a companion section file has been explicitly provided.
Use the school logo bank for exam covers. For current school exams, select the matching logo and school name rather than leaving the exam logo blank.

Do not use the Test format panel or document formatting actions for per-question layout, table details, diagram/chart settings, answer spaces, or worked-solution placement; those belong to their own structured modules.
Do not use layout-only text content when a structured block exists.
checks: No unnecessary trailing space block before a manual page break.
Working spaces correspond to intended answer locations.
Tables are classified correctly: given-data tables stay shared, while blank completion tables can be paired with solution modules as student response surfaces.
Every marked free-response item has enough student answer space for a reasonable student solution.
Student answer spaces are deliberately sized and can be manually changed by the user.
Matched student/solution slots preserve pagination while leaving normal unused space after compact solutions.
Solution-copy checks do not leave unpaired student answer spaces visible unless the teacher deliberately wants extra student-only working room with no solution.
Large nested LaTeX expressions inside fraction numerators or denominators use explicit nested \displaystyle where printable clarity matters.
Page breaks do not create large avoidable blank areas before short following content.
Title page total marks are calculated from questions, not typed manually; standard tests with sections show the calculated total for the relevant section on each section title page.
Exam booklet structure rows that describe the current document use automated question counts and marks rather than typed values.
Exam booklet preview/print output has a total page count divisible by four without content-level filler blocks; automatic extra pages should be rendered as supplementary pages.
Attachment-derived conversions preserve visible/extracted source line breaks and inline-vs-display maths unless the teacher requests a redesign.
Preview and printed PDF output should use the same visible formatting rules.

Current Student and Solutions preview reports expose total, content, and supplementary page counts plus oversized-page warnings through System Status and the agent snapshot; broad print-readiness work should inspect the required copy after it has rendered.
Document-wide layout checks have been run with mauth.layout.check for student and/or solution mode when changing broad page flow, print readiness, solution fitting, or many answer spaces at once.
